markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define backpressure, batching, and query latency goals for large repos. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeIndexPerformanceBudget leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeIndexPerformanceBudget is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 working tree: code-index implementation hardened after EventLedger JSONB idempotency false-conflict; real PostgreSQL tests green (knowledge_code_index_tests 22, knowledge_code_index:: lib 82, knowledge_code_nav_api_tests 2) plus MT-109 exact stress 25/25; adversarial re-review PASS 2026-06-14.

concerns: Real O(symbols x calls) hotspots exist and are NOT covered by the budget's enforcement: enclosing_symbol_path (relationships.rs:229) scans ALL symbols for EACH call site = O(C x S) per file; resolve_symbol_by_name (engine.rs:1033) builds+sorts a candidate vec for EACH relationship = O(R x S log S); and the nav/lens/context paths load ALL workspace symbols (O(workspace)). The mt111 fixture is ~1000 lines / ~800 symbols / ~400 calls so it touches the per-file quadratic (~320k iters, still fast), but it does NOT exercise a single LARGE file (20k+ lines) nor the O(workspace) nav cost - so the budget validates the small-file AST walk, not the actual large-repo DoS surface.

The production budget (250 ms/kloc + 50 ms) is NEVER measured against real timing: perf.rs unit tests are tautological (they assert the arithmetic 50+250*10=2550, not that any parse fits). The only timed assertion (mt111_parse_extract_within_documented_budget) runs against an 8x-inflated debug budget. So 'the production budget holds' is asserted by NO timing test; it is an unvalidated number.

The budget is per-FILE wall-clock; it cannot catch a regression that is fast per file but O(N^2) across files at workspace scale (the lens/lookup load-all pattern). The guardrail's scope is narrower than the WP constraint 'indexing large repos must stay bounded'.
evasion_probes: The delegate's honesty claim CHECKS OUT: the mt111 test comment (knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:819-826) explicitly states the production budget targets an optimized build, that the test uses an 8x debug allowance to catch quadratic blowups, and that 'the production budget itself is asserted by its own unit tests in perf.rs' (which are arithmetic-only). The admission is accurate, not concealed.

Verified is_within / allowed_ms arithmetic (perf.rs:42-50) and PerfSample::measure record a real verdict; the fixture genuinely runs extract_symbols+docs+relationships and times them (knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:835-841), so the quadratic-catch is a real (if coarse) guardrail at ~1k lines.
No micro-benchmark fraud: elapsed is measured around the actual extraction calls, not a no-op.

evidence: EventLedger idempotency false-conflict root cause confirmed as PostgreSQL JSONB payload numeric normalization while canonical payload_hash matched.
append_kernel_event idempotency now compares canonical metadata plus payload_hash instead of raw JSONB Value equality; detectors accept enriched conflict diagnostics.
Adversarial re-review PASS: no blocking findings for MT-101/102/107/109/110/111/112 readiness.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-111 (CodeIndexPerformanceBudget) defines a documented per-kloc parse+extract budget, wires a real measured verdict into the live code-index engine, and persists that verdict into a real PostgreSQL/EventLedger receipt. The implementation matches the MT contract and the current Master Spec (2.3.13.11 budget-bounded indexing), with real (not mock) PG-backed runtime proof.
findings: perf.rs:18-79 defines a real CodeIndexBudget (250 ms/kloc + 50 ms fixed) with allowed_ms/is_within arithmetic and PerfSample::measure recording a real within_budget verdict; no placeholders.

engine.rs:446-497 wraps the ACTUAL parse+extract+relationship+test-mapping pipeline (extract_symbols/extract_doc_passages/extract_relationships) in an Instant timer, computes a PerfSample against the default budget, and serializes it via perf_sample_json (engine.rs:1470-1477). The measurement is around real work, not a no-op.

engine.rs:507-530 persists perf_budget into the EventLedger receipt via append_receipt_event -> self.db.append_kernel_event (engine.rs:144-168), the real managed-PostgreSQL EventLedger append path. engine.rs:708-715 also records a code_index_perf_budget_exceeded failure_detail on the code-file row when over budget (surfaced, not silent).

Runtime proof is real: tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:1744-1800 (mt111_runtime_index_receipt_records_perf_budget_verdict) gates on knowledge_pg() (real managed PG, loud SKIP if absent, no SQLite/mock), indexes a file through the real engine, then asserts the EventLedger receipt payload carries perf_budget.within_budget==true with real elapsed_ms and allowed_ms>0.

tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:1862-1914 (mt111_parse_extract_within_documented_budget) times the real parse+extract on an ~800-symbol fixture against an 8x debug-scaled budget; the comment honestly states the production budget is asserted by perf.rs arithmetic unit tests. The honesty is explicit, not concealed.
Sub_rule_3: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, completed_by=null, status READY_FOR_VALIDATION; implementer did not self-certify COMPLETED.

Non-blocking (recorded in the implementer's own adversarial_code_review): the production budget is validated only by arithmetic unit tests, the budget is per-file wall-clock and cannot catch O(workspace) nav blowup, and no single large (20k+ line) file is exercised. MT-111 contract text requires a documented/restartable budget with PG/EventLedger authority and evidence -- NOT runtime cancellation/backpressure enforcement, a measured production-build timing assertion, or a workspace-scale guardrail -- so these are legitimate follow-up hardening, not a failure of what MT-111 contracts.
Constraints honored: no SQLite, no Docker, no external search engine/daemon, no sidecar-paper proof; storage authority is PostgreSQL/EventLedger.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194; same section numbers resolve and align. Paperwork drift only.
